headline: 19 contracts catalogued across Ethereum, Arbitrum, and Polygon; deployments on Fraxtal not yet confirmed via block explorer search.

findings.text: Etherscan name-tag search surfaced 9 Convex-labeled contracts on Ethereum: CVX token, cvxCRV token, Token Factory, CVX Locker, CRV Depositor, vlCVX Extra Rewards, cvxCRV Rewards, Multisig, and Airdrop Factory.
Arbiscan name-tag search surfaced 5 Convex-labeled contracts on Arbitrum: Booster, Reward Factory, Booster Owner, Pool Manager, and Fee Deposit.
PolygonScan name-tag search identified Booster contract (0xF403C135812408BFbE8713b5A23a04b3D48AAE31) on Polygon.

Attempted access to docs.convexfinance.com/convexfinance/overview/deployed-contracts and docs.convexfinance.com/convexfinance/faq/addresses returned permission errors; deployed-contracts doc URL not confirmed.
Audit PDF linked in protocol.audit_links exists at GitHub but PDF content not extractable via available fetch methods.
GitHub repo https://github.com/convex-eth/platform contains contracts directory structure; deployment artifacts not enumerated due to navigation limitations.
Fraxtal deployment confirmed via Medium article (April 2024) stating Convex integration with Fraxtal, but specific Fraxtal contract addresses not surfaced from block-explorer search.

rationale.verdict: Discovery slice catalogues 19 contracts confirmed by Etherscan/Arbiscan/PolygonScan name-tag searches across Ethereum, Arbitrum, and Polygon. Fraxtal presence confirmed by official blog but addresses remain unresolved via block-explorer searches. Audit PDF and detailed deployment documentation not accessible in this run.

evidence.shows: Official Convex Finance website confirms the protocol operates on Ethereum, Fraxtal, Arbitrum, and Polygon with interfaces for Curve, Frax, and f(x) Protocol boosting.
CVX Token (ERC-20) on Ethereum, verified contract, deployed by Convex Finance: Deployer
Token Factory on Ethereum, verified Convex Finance contract
CVX Locker (vICVX) on Ethereum, verified contract for CVX locking and governance voting
cvxCRV Token on Ethereum, verified ERC-20 token representing locked Curve DAO tokens
CRV Depositor on Ethereum, verified Convex Finance contract for depositing CRV
vlCVX Extra Rewards on Ethereum, verified contract distributing rewards to vlCVX holders
cvxCRV Rewards on Ethereum, verified contract holding significant cvxCRV token balances for reward distribution
Convex Finance Multisig on Ethereum, Safe-labeled multisig contract for governance
Airdrop Factory on Ethereum, verified Convex contract for merkle-tree airdrops
Booster on Arbitrum, verified Convex Finance contract managing pool interactions
Reward Factory on Arbitrum, verified Convex Finance contract creating reward pools
Booster Owner on Arbitrum, verified Convex Finance contract controlling booster parameters
Pool Manager on Arbitrum, verified Convex Finance contract managing pool additions/shutdowns
Fee Deposit on Arbitrum, Convex Finance contract for fee accumulation
Booster on Polygon, verified Convex Finance contract for pool management
Primary Convex Finance platform repository containing smart contracts for Ethereum and sidechain deployments
Convex organization with 10 repositories including platform (main), frax-cvx-platform, and sidechain-platform for multi-chain deployments
Convex Finance blog post (April 2024) confirming integration with Fraxtal blockchain and staking availability on Fraxtal

unknowns: D2: Deployed contracts documentation page (e.g., /docs/deployments or /docs/addresses) fetch blocked; canonical deployment address table not located
D3: Audit PDF content not extractable via fetch; scope/contracts-in-scope table from audit report not retrieved
D4: GitHub deployment artifacts (Foundry broadcast JSON, hardhat-deploy files, manual indexes) not enumerated; specific commit SHA not pinned
D5: Fraxtal contract addresses not surfaced from block-explorer search; Fraxtal presence confirmed by blog but on-chain verification pending
D6: Factory enumeration (child pool discovery via getPool/allPools views) not attempted; factory-created child addresses not listed

protocol_metadata.about: Convex Finance is a yield-boosting protocol that simplifies staking for Curve LP providers and CRV stakers by aggregating liquidity and distributing boosted rewards. Users deposit LP tokens or lock CRV to earn enhanced yields plus CVX tokens. The protocol operates across Ethereum, Arbitrum, Polygon, and Fraxtal, extending boosting functionality to additional DeFi platforms including Frax and f(x) Protocol.
